{{Quote|A sponsor such as ''Hoar [sic] Chall Engineering'', makers of ''Hoar [sic] Chall Ring Drive Jell?'' The lubricant that the ''champions'' rely on!|Fode|<ref name="SW14" />}}
'''Haor Chall Ring Drive Jell''' was a [[Podracer/Legends|podracer]] [[Lubricant/Legends|lubricant]] manufactured by [[Haor Chall Engineering/Legends|Haor Chall Engineering]]. The lubricant was kept in a [[Color/Legends|blue]] bottle with a green cap. The label on the bottle partially read—in the [[High Galactic/Legends|High Galactic alphabet]]—"Hoar Chall" and "Oil Gel," with another word in-between. Haor Chall and this product were one of two main sponsors for the [[Vinta Harvest Classic/Legends|Vinta Harvest Classic]] podrace on the [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]]<ref name="SW14" /> in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].<ref name="ComicsCompanion">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]''</ref> At one point during the race, announcer [[Fodesinbeed Annodue/Legends|Fodesinbeed Annodue]] advertised the product, with Fode declaring it "the lubricant that the champions rely on."<ref name="SW14" /> During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Haor Chall was compromised following the destruction of its [[Factory/Legends|factories]] on the planet [[Charros IV/Legends|Charros IV]] at the hands of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. As a result, the company [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] absorbed Haor Chall alongside the patent for the Ring Drive Jell.<ref name="TNEGVV">''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Haor Chall Ring Drive Jell first appeared in the [[Star Wars (1998) 14|fourteenth issue]]—written by [[Timothy Truman]] and penciled by [[Tom Lyle]]—of the [[1998]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'',<ref name="SW14" /> which was published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[January 26]], [[2000]].<ref name="DarkHorse">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/99-102/Star-Wars-14Emissaries-to-Malastare-Part-2-of-6|text=Star Wars #14: Emissaries to Malastare (Part 2 of 6)}}</ref> In the issue, the bottle held by Annodue reads "gel" instead of "jell."<ref name="SW14" /> As the [[2003]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''—written by [[W. Haden Blackman]]—re-uses "jell," this article assumes it is the correct spelling.<ref name="TNEGVV" /> Additionally, ''Star Wars'' 14 spells the first word of the product "Hoar" rather than "Haor."<ref name="SW14" /> As ''The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels'' uses the "Haor" spelling, which is in turn consistent with the name of the product's manufacturer, Haor Chall Engineering, this article assumes it is the correct spelling.<ref name="TNEGVV" />
